text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST
MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT GADOLINIUM CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION: 57-year-old female with history of Stage IV metastatic cutaneous melanoma (BRAF V600E-mutant), primary left posterior shoulder. Previously treated with dabrafenib/trametinib (first-line, 8 months), then ipilimumab/nivolumab combination immunotherapy with transition to nivolumab 480 mg IV every 4 weeks maintenance (total approximately 2 years of checkpoint inhibitor therapy). Therapy electively discontinued following confirmed near-complete to complete radiographic and pathologic response. This study represents scheduled surveillance imaging approximately 6 months following the final dose of nivolumab. Prior imaging demonstrated near-complete response with single subcentimeter right hepatic lobe lesion (0.4 cm), and prior liver biopsy demonstrating no viable melanoma cells at that site. Evaluate for disease recurrence.

COMPARISON: CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast (prior study, approximately 6 months prior); MRI brain with and without gadolinium contrast (prior study, approximately 6 months prior).

TECHNIQUE:
CT: Multidetector helical CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed following the uneventful administration of 100 mL of Omnipaque-350 intravenous contrast material. Acquisitions obtained in portal venous phase. Multiplanar reformatted images reconstructed in axial, coronal, and sagittal planes. Slice thickness 5 mm (chest) and 3 mm (abdomen/pelvis) with sub-millimeter reconstructions reviewed at the workstation.
MRI Brain: Standard multiparametric brain MRI protocol including pre- and post-gadolinium sequences. Axial T1, T2, FLAIR, and diffusion-weighted imaging obtained. Post-contrast T1-weighted sequences obtained following administration of 7.5 mL Gadavist intravenous gadolinium-based contrast agent. No acute complications.

---

CT CHEST FINDINGS:

Lungs and Airways: The lungs are well-aerated bilaterally. No pleural effusion. No pneumothorax. The previously described two small right lower lobe pulmonary nodules (measuring 0.3 cm and 0.2 cm on the prior study) are not identified on the current examination. This most likely represents continued treatment response and resolution, as these nodules had progressively decreased in size over serial imaging and were below CT resolution on the prior study as well. No new pulmonary nodules or masses are identified. The airways are patent to the segmental level bilaterally. No endobronchial lesions.

Mediastinum and Hila: The mediastinum is midline. No anterior mediastinal, paratracheal, subcarinal, or hilar lymphadenopathy. The aortopulmonary window is clear. Cardiac silhouette is normal in size. No pericardial effusion.

Axilla and Chest Wall: Bilateral axillary regions examined. No discrete lymphadenopathy identified in either axilla. On the right, the previously described axillary lymph node which measured up to 2.1 cm at diagnosis and subsequently decreased to 0.4 cm on the prior study is no longer identified as a discrete abnormal node. Similarly, no pathologically enlarged left axillary lymph nodes are seen. The chest wall soft tissues are unremarkable. No evidence of subcutaneous nodules or masses in the chest wall or shoulder regions. The previously excised left posterior shoulder primary site shows expected post-surgical changes without evidence of local recurrence or new soft tissue masses in this region.

Bony Thorax: The ribs, sternum, clavicles, and thoracic spine are intact without lytic or sclerotic lesions to suggest osseous metastatic disease. No pathologic fractures.

---

CT ABDOMEN/PELVIS FINDINGS:

Liver: The liver is normal in size and contour. The hepatic parenchyma demonstrates homogeneous attenuation without focal lesion. The previously documented right hepatic lobe lesion measuring 0.4 cm on the prior study — which at its largest measured 3.1 cm at initial staging — is not identified on this examination. No new focal hepatic lesions are seen. There is no evidence of capsular irregularity or morphologic change to suggest hepatic involvement by disease. No intrahepatic biliary ductal dilatation. The portal vein is patent.

Gallbladder and Biliary System: Gallbladder is normal in size and wall thickness. No gallstones. Common bile duct is not dilated.

Spleen: Normal size and attenuation. No splenic lesions.

Pancreas: Normal in size, contour, and attenuation throughout. No pancreatic ductal dilatation. No peripancreatic fluid or mass.

Adrenal Glands: Bilateral adrenal glands are normal in size and configuration. No adrenal nodules or masses. No evidence of adrenal metastases.

Kidneys and Ureters: The kidneys are symmetric, normal in size, and enhance uniformly. No solid renal masses. No hydronephrosis. No hydroureter. Incidental note is made of a simple-appearing renal cyst measuring approximately 1.3 cm in the upper pole of the left kidney, stable and unchanged from prior imaging, consistent with a benign simple cyst (Bosniak I).

Retroperitoneum: No retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy. The aorta is normal in caliber throughout. No para-aortic or aortocaval lymph node enlargement.

Pelvis: The uterus and ovaries are within normal limits for age. No pelvic lymphadenopathy. No pelvic masses. No free fluid in the pelvis.

Gastrointestinal Tract: The stomach is decompressed. Visualized small and large bowel loops are within normal limits without wall thickening or dilatation. No mesenteric masses. No ascites. Given the patient's history of prior grade 3 immune-mediated colitis treated with high-dose steroids, the colonic wall is reviewed carefully — the colonic wall appears thin and normal throughout without residual wall thickening or mesenteric stranding. This is consistent with complete clinical and imaging resolution of prior colitis.

Soft Tissues and Subcutaneous: The soft tissues of the abdomen, flanks, and pelvis are unremarkable. Importantly, no subcutaneous nodules are identified in the right flank — the site of previously documented subcutaneous metastatic disease at diagnosis (measuring up to 1.4 cm). The flanks and anterior abdominal wall are clear. No new soft tissue masses.

Bony Pelvis and Lumbar Spine: The lumbar spine and pelvic bones are intact without suspicious focal lesions. No evidence of osseous metastases.

---

MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT GADOLINIUM FINDINGS:

Brain Parenchyma: The cerebral hemispheres are symmetric. Gray-white matter differentiation is well preserved. No focal areas of abnormal signal intensity on T1, T2, or FLAIR sequences to suggest parenchymal metastatic deposits. No leptomeningeal enhancement is identified on post-contrast sequences. No areas of abnormal post-gadolinium parenchymal enhancement are seen to suggest metastatic disease. No cortical or subcortical signal abnormality. No evidence of surrounding vasogenic edema.

Extra-axial Spaces: No extra-axial collections. No subdural or epidural hematomas. The sulci are symmetric and within normal limits for the patient's age. Minimal non-specific periventricular T2/FLAIR signal changes, felt to represent early small vessel ischemic change in the context of the patient's prior smoking history and age — no change from prior.

Ventricles: The ventricular system is symmetric and normal in size. No hydrocephalus. The fourth ventricle is midline and patent.

Posterior Fossa: The cerebellum and brainstem are unremarkable. No cerebellar or brainstem lesions. The cranial nerves are not encased or displaced.

Calvarium and Skull Base: No calvarial lytic or sclerotic lesions. The skull base is intact. No evidence of osseous metastases in the calvarium or skull base.

Orbit and Soft Tissues: Not the primary focus of this study; no gross abnormality identified in the limited field of view.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. No evidence of intrathoracic, abdominal, pelvic, or intracranial metastatic disease. The previously documented sites of metastatic melanoma — right flank subcutaneous nodule, bilateral axillary lymphadenopathy, bilateral hepatic metastases, and right lower lobe pulmonary nodules — have all resolved without any identifiable residual lesion on today's study. This represents an ongoing and durable complete radiographic response, now confirmed at approximately 6 months following elective discontinuation of nivolumab after 2 years of total checkpoint inhibitor therapy.

2. The right hepatic lobe lesion measuring 0.4 cm on the prior study, previously biopsied and confirmed to contain no viable melanoma (consistent with treated/regressed disease), is not identified on this examination, consistent with complete resolution.

3. No new sites of metastatic disease in any anatomic compartment. No new lymphadenopathy, no new pulmonary nodules, no new hepatic lesions, no new soft tissue or osseous lesions.

4. No intracranial metastases on dedicated MRI brain with gadolinium.

5. Incidental stable simple left renal cyst (Bosniak I), not of clinical concern.

6. CT abdomen without evidence of residual or recurrent colitis, consistent with complete resolution of prior grade 3 immune-mediated colitis.

Overall, the imaging findings are consistent with complete and durable systemic response following completion of definitive checkpoint inhibitor therapy for Stage IV metastatic melanoma.

Reporting Radiologist: [Attending Radiologist, Oncologic Imaging]
Dictated and electronically signed.
